slides.title: Key findings (2/2) - Adjusted EBITDA bridge
slides.strapline: Bridge walkthrough and phase-by-phase commentary for management discussion.

analysisColumns.heading: FY21 to FY22, Adjusted EBITDA:
body: An increase in revenue from FY21 to FY22 was driven by recovery in consumer and tourism spend, which increased transaction volumes.
Variable expenses scaled with revenue, while compensation and software costs increased with higher activity and staffing.
Management also highlighted volume mix shifts toward higher-yield transactions, which supported gross margin progression in select channels.
Temporary project staffing and incremental systems work contributed to a higher run-rate cost base entering FY23.
Working-capital timing did not materially affect adjusted EBITDA in this period but created noise in monthly operating reviews.
The net result was strong earnings growth with early signals of operating leverage despite ongoing investment in capabilities.

analysisColumns.heading: FY22 to FY23, Adjusted EBITDA:
body: Processing services revenue growth reflected switching volume and incremental professional services engagements.
Operating expenses increased from contracted support capacity and implementation activity.
Professional services demand was concentrated in complex client migrations that required additional delivery oversight.
Cost growth partially reflected wage inflation and selective backfills in critical operational teams.
Product and platform enhancements contributed to recurring amortization and support spend in the year.
Overall profitability expanded, though less than revenue growth, as investment and scaling costs were absorbed.

analysisColumns.heading: FY23 to TTM Jun-24, Adjusted EBITDA:
body: Revenue increased on higher transaction volume and expanding payment product adoption.
Margin progression remained supported by volume-led operating leverage.
Management reported continued expansion in priority customer cohorts and steadier retention outcomes.
Near-term margin variability was influenced by launch costs tied to newer product initiatives.
Cash collections remained healthy and supported working-capital discipline despite growth-related complexity.
The period ended with a stronger EBITDA baseline and improved visibility into forward operating performance.

slides.source: Source: Management information and audited financial statements.
slides.note: Calculated based on period-end operating metrics and management bridge commentary.
